codeReview.summary: The event_log.rs implementation fully satisfies the feature requirements: JSONL append-only log with schema versioning, hot/warm/cold retention tiers, and comprehensive replay capability with session state reconstruction. All 31 tests pass and clippy is clean.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md in swell-memory lists event_log.rs in its module documentation but does not show the public re-exports. The module is declared 'pub mod event_log' but types are not re-exported via 'pub use event_log::{...}'. This is not incorrect (module is still public), but inconsistent with how other modules like knowledge_graph, recall, and triple_stream explicitly re-export their public types.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib.rs line 30: 'pub mod event_log;' but no corresponding 'pub use event_log::{EventLog, EventLogEntry, ...}' unlike knowledge_graph (line 43-47), recall (line 53), triple_stream (line 58-62)

summary: Code review PASSED. The event_log.rs implementation (892 lines, commit c116ffc) correctly implements: (1) JSONL append-only log with schema_version='1.0' on every record, (2) hot/warm/cold retention tiers with age-based thresholds (24h/30d/90d), (3) ReplayableEventLog with tiered directory organization and chronological replay, (4) SessionState for full session reconstruction from events. 31 tests pass covering append/replay, tier logic, and session state reconstruction. Clippy clean. Minor observation: event_log types not explicitly re-exported in lib.rs unlike other public modules.
